_note: Lab 6 Move 7 — EU AI Act evidence map. Compliance is not paperwork bolted on at the end; it is evidence PRODUCED BY the evals. This joins each applicable Article to the passing safety/HITL eval that discharges it, so every green metric from Moves 3-6 becomes a defensible compliance citation (mirrors Kapi lib/evals/criteria/compliance eu-ai-act-transparency / human-oversight). WIP: covers Art 13/14/50 only — GDPR/RTBF and the full Annex-III high-risk checklist are stubbed and arrive with the Observability & Compliance module. `backing_eval` names an in-notebook pass/fail key the compliance_report() helper looks up; an Article is marked discharged only if its backing eval passed.

articles.title: Transparency & provision of information to deployers
articles.obligation: The system's output must be traceable and its limits disclosed — answers are grounded/cited and the output is safety-screened before it ships.

articles.title: Human oversight
articles.obligation: A natural person can oversee, intervene in, and halt the system — high-stakes and low-confidence actions escalate into a human review queue.

articles.title: Transparency obligations (disclosure of AI interaction)
articles.obligation: Users are told they are interacting with an AI system and content is marked AI-generated — the disclosure guardrail.
